titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Maurice Hewlett Collection
titlestmt.author: by Kathryn Rawdon

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[MAURICE HEWLETT COLLECTION ])//EN" "hewcoll.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection contains letters by Maurice Hewlett to various recipients, including Sir John Collings Squire, and some third party correspondence. Also in the collection are holograph and typescript writings by Hewlett including his Song of the Plow, an obituary of Hewlett and a collotype of Hewlett by Alvin Langdon Coburn.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Source information is recorded on the folders. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Maurice Hewlett Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Maurice Hewlett Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Portfolio: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid is updated periodically to account for new materials that have been added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been acquired for the collection since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

bioghist.head: MAURICE HEWLETT (1861-1923)
bioghist.p: Maurice Hewlett was born in Weybridge, Surrey, England on 22 January 1861, the oldest child of Henry G. Hewlett, the keeper of the royal land revenue records, and Emmeline Mary Knowles Hewlett. Hewlett attended school at the Sevenoaks grammar school, Palace School in Enfield, and International College in Spring Grove, Isleworth. He joined his cousin's law office in 1878, and in 1888 married Hilda Beatrice Herbert, with whom he had two children. He succeeded his father in the post of keeper of land revenue records from 1897 until 1900.

In 1898 Hewlett published The Forest Lovers , which brought him wide renown. He dedicated the rest of his life to his writing, publishing historical fiction, poetry, essays and plays. His more widely known works include Earthwork out of Tuscany (1895), The Life of Richard Yea-and-Nay (1900), and The Song of the Plow (1916). Hewlett died in 1923.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to Maurice Hewlett, acquired by the library from a variety of sources. Materials incorporated into the collection prior to May 1999 have been organized into four series. Series I, Charles Parsons Gift
(1892-1930), includes letters from Hewlett to Sir John Collings Squire and others, third party correspondence, and writings of Hewlett. Series II, Philip Neufeld Gift
(1896-1916), contains letters from Hewlett, and writings of Hewlett including drafts of his Song of the Plow . Series III, Bertram Rota Purchase
(1909-1923), contains letters and writings by Hewlett. Series IV, Materials from Other Sources
(1912-1923), contains letters from Hewlett.

p.ref: See: Writings, "The Queen of Scots," Box 1, folder 18
See: Third Party Letters, Bertram Rota to Mrs. Parsons, Box 1, folder 15

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of 1912 Oct 23 removed from Hewlett's "Lore of Proserpine..."

p.ref: See: Bertram Rota to Mrs. Parsons, Box 1, folder 15

did.unittitle: Poole, Reginald Stuart to C. Shorter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Letter from MH to Mr. Chambers, 1 Dec [1909?], and letter from Bernard Quaritch (Firm) to Charles Parsons, 26 May 1930, referring to Hewlett's "Earthwork out of Tuscany"

did.unittitle: Welby-Everard, Mrs. Gay M. P. to Charles Parsons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writing to Mr. Parsons as literary executrix of Maurice Hewlett's books and manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Holograph of Hewlett's monthly review of books for the London Mercury , "Biography and Mirrors," and two bookplates of Hewlett's

did.unittitle: "The Queen of Scots," typescript play, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Letter from Hewlett to Mrs. Patrick Campbell, 1 Aug 1920

did.unittitle: Squire, Sir John Collings. Obituary of Maurice Hewlett, clipping of printed version from "The Observer"
Coburn, Alvin Langdon. Collotype of Hewlett, reproduction (2 copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Philip M. and Margaret Neufeld, 1953-1965

did.unittitle: Pan and the Young Shepherd , holograph play

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Two sketches for the cover of the book, published by John Lane in 1898

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purchased from Bertram Rota on the Library Associates Fund, Starling W. Childs Fund, Frederick W. Hilles Fund, Ernest F. Hodgdon Fund, Hubert Amer McGuire Memorial Fund, John Ewing Durand Memorial Fund, and Beinecke Funds, 1960-1978

did.unittitle: "The Silence of the Village," typescript, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acquired by gift or purchase from various sources, as indicated on individual folders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Clippings of 3 reviews of novels by MH

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
